name: Badge
description: A badge is a small indicator placed on another element to show a count or status — the number of unread alerts on the notifications bell, or a dot marking a vehicle that needs attention.
keyRule: Use a badge to draw attention to a count or new state on an icon or item. Keep it glanceable — for detailed status use a status chip instead.

variants.name: Standard (numeric)
variants.whenToUse: Show a count via `badgeContent` (unread alerts, pending tasks).

variants.name: Capped
variants.whenToUse: Large counts automatically render as "99+" once `badgeContent` exceeds `max` (default 99).

anatomy.name: Host
anatomy.description: the `children` — the icon/element the badge is anchored to.

anatomy.name: Badge
anatomy.description: the small count or dot indicator, positioned via `anchorOrigin` (default top-right).

anatomy.name: Content
anatomy.description: `badgeContent` — a number, auto-capped value ("99+"), or nothing (dot variant).

behaviors.name: Capping
behaviors.description: `max` (default 99) caps large counts so the badge stays small and readable.

behaviors.name: Zero
behaviors.description: Badge auto-hides when `badgeContent` is 0 unless `showZero` is true.

behaviors.name: Live updates
behaviors.description: When the count changes, update it promptly; pair with a live region on the host if the change should be announced to assistive tech (Badge itself does not add one).

states.treatment: Default `color="default"` (grey) pill/dot on the host; Cartrack usage commonly sets `color="error"` for alert counts.
`invisible` prop or an auto-hidden zero count removes the badge from view (still rendered in the DOM, visually hidden).

specs.value: 20px min-width/height pill, border-radius 10px, 0/6px padding (MUI's built-in geometry, not theme-shape-derived)
`color="default"` (grey) by default; `error` (#F44336) with white text is the common alert-count usage and passes AA
Inherits surrounding font-size; no dedicated Badge typography variant. semantic.typography.scale.labelSmall (12px/500) is the closest named scale step visually, but is not a real MUI `variant`.

props.name: children
props.description: The host element (icon/button) the badge is anchored to.

props.name: badgeContent
props.description: The content rendered within the badge (a number in most cases).

props.name: color
props.description: Badge colour. Default is 'default' (grey); Cartrack commonly uses 'error' for alert counts.

props.name: variant
props.description: 'standard' shows badgeContent; 'dot' renders a small dot with no number.

props.name: max
props.description: Max count to show before capping to "{max}+". Default 99.

props.name: showZero
props.description: Controls whether the badge is hidden when badgeContent is zero. Default false — badge hides at zero.

props.name: invisible
props.description: Forces the badge invisible regardless of content. Default false.

props.name: anchorOrigin
props.description: Where the badge sits relative to its host. Default { vertical: 'top', horizontal: 'right' }.

props.name: overlap
props.description: Shape of the wrapped host, affects badge positioning math. Default 'rectangular'.

doThis: Cap large counts (99+ is automatic via `max`).
Use `variant="dot"` when a number isn't needed.
Give the host element (not the Badge) its own accessible name that reflects the count, e.g. aria-label="Notifications, 4 unread".
dontDoThis: Don't put long text in `badgeContent` — it's sized for a number or a dot.
Don't rely on the badge alone to convey a critical state — pair with text/colour elsewhere.
accessibility: Badge has no built-in accessible label — the host's accessible name should include the badge meaning (e.g. "Notifications, 4 unread").
There is no invented `hostLabel` prop; naming the host is the consumer's responsibility via aria-label/aria-labelledby on the host element.
The dot/number is not the only signal of an important state.

related.name: Chip
related.why: When the label needs words, not just a count

related.name: Tooltip
related.why: Icon-only targets with a badge still need a tooltip
